title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 15 (Fragment 2)
text:
“But she’s terrified of heights!” said Angelica. “How did she get this high?”

Vittoria told Angelica she was to get Renata up at once.

Angelica stuck her upper half out between the pillars. Being small certainly had its advantages. Distances which were mercilessly huge to Renata and Paolo were too far away to worry Angelica. The dome was like a whole small world to her.

Paolo said, carefully patient, “I can’t hold on much longer. Do you think you can have an-other try?” The answer from Renata was a sobbing shudder.

“Renata!” shouted Angelica.

Renata’s scared face turned slowly up. “Some thing’s happened to my eyes now! You look tiny.”

“I am tiny!” yelled Angelica.

“Both of them are!” Paolo said, staring at Tonino’s head.

“Pull me up quick,” said Renata. The size of Angelica and Tonino so worried Renata and Paolo that both of them forgot they were hundreds of feet in the air. Paolo heaved on Renata and Renata shoved at Paolo, and they scrambled over the marble rail in a second. But there, Renata looked up at the immense golden Angel and had an instant relapse. “Oh—oh!” she wailed and sank down in a heap against the golden pedestal.

Tonino and Angelica huddled behind her. The warmth of climbing had worn off. They were feeling the wind keenly through their scanty nightshirts.

Benvenuto leaped across Renata to them. Some thing else had to be done, and done quickly.

Tonino went again and looked through the marble pillars, where the dome curved away and down like an ice field with ribs of green and gold. There, coming into view over the curve, was a bright red uniform, making Marco’s carroty hair look faded and sallow against it. The uniform went with Marco’s hair even less well than the crimson he had worn as a coachman. Tonino knew who Marco was in that instant. But that bothered him less than seeing Marco flattened to the surface and looking backwards, which Tonino was sure was a mistake. Beyond Marco’s boots, fair hair was wildly blowing. Rosa’s flushed face came into sight.

“I’m all right . Look after yourself,” Rosa said.

Benvenuto was beside Tonino. They were to come up quicker than that. It was important.

“Get Rosa and Marco up here quickly!” Tonino shrieked to Paolo. He did not know if he had caught the feeling from the cats or not, but he was sure Rosa and Marco were in danger.

Paolo went unwillingly to the railing and flinched at the height. “They’ve been following us and shouting the whole way,” he said. “Get up here quickly!” he shouted.

“Thank you very much!” Marco shouted back. “Whose fault is it we’re up here anyway?”

“Is Renata all right?” Rosa yelled.

Angelica and Tonino pushed themselves between the pillars. “Hurry up!” they screamed.

The sight of them worked on Rosa and Marco as it had done on Renata and Paolo. They stared at the two tiny figures, and got to their feet as they stared. Then, stooping over, with their hands hanging, they came racing up the last of the curve for a closer look. Marco tumbled over the rail and said, as he was pulling Rosa over, “I couldn’t believe my eyes at first! We’d better do a growing-spell before—”

“Get down!” said Paolo. Benvenuto’s message was so urgent that he had caught it too. Both cats were crouching, stiff and low, and even Benvenuto’s flat ears were flattened. Rosa stooped down. Marco grudgingly went on one knee.

“Look here, Paolo—” he began.

A savage gale hit the dome. Freezing wind shrieked across the platform, howled in the spaces between the marble pillars, and scoured across the curve of the dome below. The Angel’s wings thrummed with it. It brought stabs of rain and needles of ice, hurling so hard that Tonino was thrown flat on his face. He could hear ice rattling on the Angel and spitting on the dome. Paolo snatched him into shelter behind himself. Renata feebly scrabbled about until she found Angelica and dragged her into shelter by one arm. Marco and Rosa bowed over. It was quite clear that anyone climbing the dome would have been blown off.

vocabulary.definition: of a rich deep red color inclining to purple
vocabulary.example: The uniform went with Marco’s hair even less well than the crimson he had worn as a coachman.

vocabulary.definition: lowering the body close to the ground by bending the knees
vocabulary.example: Both cats were crouching, stiff and low, and even Benvenuto’s flat ears were flattened.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that lacks strength or force
vocabulary.example: Renata feebly scrabbled about until she found Angelica and dragged her into shelter by one arm.

vocabulary.definition: pressed or smashed so as to become flat
vocabulary.example: But that bothered him less than seeing Marco flattened to the surface and looking backwards, which Tonino was sure was a mistake.

vocabulary.definition: make a quick, nervous movement as an instinctive reaction to fear
vocabulary.example: Paolo went unwillingly to the railing and flinched at the height.

vocabulary.definition: in an unwilling or reluctant manner
vocabulary.example: Marco grudgingly went on one knee.

vocabulary.definition: to make a loud, prolonged cry, typically of pain, anger, or excitement
vocabulary.example: Freezing wind shrieked across the platform, howled in the spaces between the marble pillars, and scoured across the curve of the dome below.

vocabulary.definition: crowded or gathered closely together
vocabulary.example: Tonino and Angelica huddled behind her.

vocabulary.definition: extremely large or great in size or degree
vocabulary.example: But there, Renata looked up at the immense golden Angel and had an instant relapse.

vocabulary.definition: in an intense or highly focused manner
vocabulary.example: They were feeling the wind keenly through their scanty nightshirts.

vocabulary.definition: a railing or barrier made of marble
vocabulary.example: Paolo heaved on Renata and Renata shoved at Paolo, and they scrambled over the marble rail in a second.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that shows no mercy or pity
vocabulary.example: Distances which were mercilessly huge to Renata and Paolo were too far away to worry Angelica.

vocabulary.definition: able to accept or tolerate delays or problems without becoming annoyed
vocabulary.example: Paolo said, carefully patient, “I can’t hold on much longer.

vocabulary.definition: the base or support on which a statue is mounted
vocabulary.example: ” she wailed and sank down in a heap against the golden pedestal.

vocabulary.definition: vertical structural supports
vocabulary.example: Angelica stuck her upper half out between the pillars.

vocabulary.definition: a deterioration after a period of improvement
vocabulary.example: But there, Renata looked up at the immense golden Angel and had an instant relapse.

vocabulary.definition: an extremely strong and fierce windstorm
vocabulary.example: A savage gale hit the dome.

vocabulary.definition: to move hurriedly or clumsily from one place to another
vocabulary.example: Paolo heaved on Renata and Renata shoved at Paolo, and they scrambled over the marble rail in a second.

vocabulary.definition: to utter a high-pitched piercing sound or words, especially as an expression of terror or pain
vocabulary.example: ” Tonino shrieked to Paolo.

vocabulary.definition: a sudden convulsive movement, typically as a result of fear or cold
vocabulary.example: ” The answer from Renata was a sobbing shudder.

vocabulary.definition: quickly seize something in a rude or eager way
vocabulary.example: Paolo snatched him into shelter behind himself.

vocabulary.definition: crying noisily with convulsive catches of the breath
vocabulary.example: ” The answer from Renata was a sobbing shudder.

vocabulary.definition: a convulsive trembling caused by crying deeply
vocabulary.example: ” The answer from Renata was a sobbing shudder.

vocabulary.definition: falling in small drops (like raindrops or saliva)
vocabulary.example: He could hear ice rattling on the Angel and spitting on the dome.

vocabulary.definition: bending one's head or body forward and downward
vocabulary.example: Then, stooping over, with their hands hanging, they came racing up the last of the curve for a closer look.

vocabulary.definition: feeling extreme fear or anxiety
vocabulary.example: “But she’s terrified of heights!

vocabulary.definition: distinctive clothing worn by members of the same organization or group
vocabulary.example: There, coming into view over the curve, was a bright red uniform, making Marco’s carroty hair look faded and sallow against it.

vocabulary.definition: requiring immediate action or attention
vocabulary.example: Benvenuto’s message was so urgent that he had caught it too.
